steps.input_summary: SWIFT rail health degrading — p95 latency 3,847ms on sanctions-svc
steps.finding_summary: Anomaly detected: sanctions-svc timeout rate 47x baseline. Circuit breaker HALF_OPEN on OFAC-SCSN-EU-01. 847 payments queuing across USD-EUR corridor.
steps.static_narrative: The Sentinel Agent detected a critical anomaly in the USD-EUR corridor at 23:17 EST. Sanctions screening latency crossed 3,800ms — 47 times the baseline — triggering a circuit breaker on the OFAC SCSN EU node. 847 high-value payments from Siemens AG Treasury, Rio Tinto Commodities, and Merck FX Desk are now queuing behind the CashPro / GPP Hub gateway.

steps.input_summary: 847 payment failures + FTS-2000 (COBOL) adapter telemetry + MQ depth signals
steps.finding_summary: Root cause chain identified: sanctions-svc connection pool saturated → FTS-2000 (COBOL) interpreting timeouts as soft-fail → 3x retry amplification → queue depth 2,847.
steps.business_signal: Retry storm: FTS-2000 (COBOL) generating 3x message volume
steps.static_narrative: Cross-referencing 847 payment traces with FTS-2000 (COBOL) adapter telemetry reveals the amplification chain. The sanctions service connection pool (50 threads) is fully saturated. FTS-2000 (COBOL) — the bank's COBOL-era settlement adapter — interprets every timeout as a retryable soft failure and re-submits 3 times per message. This turned a linear latency spike into an exponential queue growth event. The CashPro / GPP Hub message queue has grown from 12 to 2,847 messages.

steps.input_summary: 12,847 log entries from sanctions-svc, FTS-2000 (COBOL), MQ broker, fx-engine
steps.finding_summary: 4 semantic clusters: sanctions-timeout (38%), FTS-2000 (COBOL) retry-noise (41%), queue-overflow (15%), fx-staleness (6%). Retry noise masks root cause in manual triage.
steps.business_signal: 41% of logs are retry noise masking root cause
steps.static_narrative: Semantic clustering of 12,847 log entries reveals the signal-to-noise problem. FTS-2000 (COBOL) retry noise constitutes 41% of all log volume — in a traditional SOC, analysts would spend hours filtering these before reaching the sanctions root cause (38%). The AI semantic engine cut through this in 2.3 seconds. A secondary FX rate staleness cluster (6%) indicates the USD/EUR rate cache is aging beyond threshold, creating a downstream risk for any payments that do clear.

steps.input_summary: Clustered failure patterns + active payment records + client SLA data
steps.finding_summary: Affected cohort: 1,247 payments across 3 clients. Siemens AG Treasury: 487 ($74M). Rio Tinto Commodities: 412 ($63M). Merck FX Desk: 348 ($45M). All stuck in sanctions-check-pending.
steps.business_signal: 3 Tier-1 clients affected: Siemens AG Treasury, Rio Tinto Commodities, Merck FX Desk
steps.static_narrative: Complete cohort identification: 1,247 payments totaling $182M across three institutional clients. Siemens AG Treasury has the largest exposure at $74M with a time-critical batch sweep that must settle before the Tokyo FX window. Rio Tinto Commodities has $63M in queued energy hedging payments. Merck FX Desk has $45M in supply chain settlements. All 1,247 payments are frozen in sanctions-check-pending state with BIC BOFAUS3N as originator.

steps.input_summary: 1,247-payment cohort + FX window schedules + regulatory thresholds
steps.finding_summary: Total value at risk: $182M. FX settlement window closes in 28 min. GPI SLA breach on all 1,247 payments. Regulatory reporting trigger if >$100M delayed >60min.
steps.business_signal: SLA breach: 1,247 payments, $182M, 28 min to FX close
steps.static_narrative: Business impact is severe and time-critical. $182M at risk with a hard deadline — the FX settlement window closes at 23:45 UTC (28 minutes). All 1,247 payments are in GPI SLA breach. The OCC supervised · DFAST · Basel III LCR framework triggers mandatory regulatory reporting if >$100M remains delayed beyond 60 minutes. Manual alternative: estimated 72 ops-hours across three regional investigation teams, well beyond the window.

steps.input_summary: Impact assessment + CashPro / GPP Hub topology + redundancy paths
steps.finding_summary: 3 remediation options generated. Recommended: Reroute via REFINITIV-WCO-US-01 (speed 9/10, risk 3/10). Backup vendor verified healthy — 99.97% uptime.
steps.business_signal: Recommended: reroute via backup sanctions provider, est. 4 min
steps.static_narrative: Evaluated CashPro / GPP Hub topology and identified three remediation paths. Top recommendation: reroute all 1,247 queued payments through REFINITIV-WCO-US-01 (secondary sanctions vendor). This secondary provider has maintained 99.97% uptime over the past 30 days and has spare capacity. Critically, this is not a sanctions bypass — all payments still undergo full OFAC+EU+UK screening through an equivalent provider. Estimated 4-minute switchover including queue drain.

steps.input_summary: 3 remediation options with risk/speed/impact tradeoff matrix
steps.finding_summary: HUMAN APPROVAL REQUIRED. Value ($182M) exceeds autonomous threshold ($50M). Escalating to authorized operator. Countdown: FX window 24 min.
steps.static_narrative: Under OCC supervised · DFAST · Basel III LCR governance framework, remediation actions affecting >$50M require human-in-the-loop approval. The $182M exposure is 3.6x the autonomous threshold. Escalating to the HITL cockpit with all three options, tradeoff analysis, and the 24-minute countdown. The Governance Agent has pre-validated Option A against the bank's compliance rulebook — no sanctions bypass, all screening maintained through equivalent vendor.

steps.input_summary: Approved: Option A — reroute via REFINITIV-WCO-US-01
steps.finding_summary: Executing 6-step recovery. Queue redirected. FTS-2000 (COBOL) retry cycle halted. Draining 1,247 payments at 52 payments/min. Circuit breaker transitioning CLOSED.
steps.business_signal: Recovery in progress: 52 payments/min drain rate
steps.static_narrative: Recovery sequence executing. REFINITIV-WCO-US-01 activated and confirmed healthy. Message queue redirected from OFAC-SCSN-EU-01 to secondary vendor. FTS-2000 (COBOL) retry cycle halted — adapter set to single-attempt mode. Currently draining 1,247 queued payments at 52 payments per minute through the backup screening path. Circuit breaker on primary sanctions node transitioning to CLOSED as load is removed. Queue depth falling: 2,847 → 1,900 → 1,100.

steps.input_summary: Recovery execution logs + settlement confirmations + GPI tracker
steps.finding_summary: Recovery verified: 1,247/1,247 payments cleared. STP rate restored to 98%. MTTR: 11 min (vs 90 min manual). FX window met with 17 min to spare.
steps.business_signal: RESOLVED: 1,247 payments cleared, $182M protected, 17 min to spare
steps.static_narrative: Final verification complete. All 1,247 payments have been processed through REFINITIV-WCO-US-01 and confirmed settled via GPI tracker. STP rate restored from 71% to 98%. Mean time to resolution: 11 minutes — compared to the estimated 90-minute manual baseline, that's an 88% improvement. The FX settlement window was met with 17 minutes to spare. FTS-2000 (COBOL) has resumed normal retry behavior. Circuit breaker on OFAC-SCSN-EU-01 now CLOSED. Full 9-step audit trail generated for OCC supervised · DFAST · Basel III LCR compliance review.

remediation_options.title: Reroute via backup sanctions provider
remediation_options.description: Switch OFAC screening to REFINITIV-WCO-US-01 (secondary vendor). Estimated 3min switchover. Full compliance screening maintained through equivalent provider.

remediation_options.title: Pause batch + retry at 06:00 EST
remediation_options.description: Hold all queued payments. Schedule batch re-submission at 06:00 EST when sanctions service has been restarted. Misses current FX window.

remediation_options.title: Isolate affected cohort + manual review queue
remediation_options.description: Quarantine 1,247 stuck payments. Route to manual review queue for Ops team triage. Partial recovery possible within 45 min.
